name: GPT-4 Turbo
note: 128K-context, cheaper GPT-4 announced at OpenAI's first DevDay; current default snapshot gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09 carries a Dec 2023 knowledge cutoff.

results.notes: MT-Bench score for gpt-4-1106-preview (the GPT-4 Turbo snapshot). MT-Bench is an LMSYS-run, GPT-4-judged multi-turn evaluation (a third-party benchmark run, not an OpenAI self-report), relayed here via the same Telnyx model page already used and independently confirmed (via direct fetch) as the source for this file's existing MMLU and Arena Elo rows.

gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09 (the snapshot this file's note field identifies as GPT-4 Turbo's current default), 0-shot CoT, 'assistant' system prompt. From OpenAI's own simple-evals repo — the accuracy table OpenAI publishes to be transparent about numbers accompanying its model launches; this row is the GPT-4 Turbo comparison point first published alongside the GPT-4o ('Hello GPT-4o') launch.
gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09, 0-shot CoT. Classic MATH test set (OpenAI's simple-evals only swaps to the newer MATH-500 split for o1-and-later models, so this is the original benchmark, not a variant).

gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09, 0-shot, OpenAI simple-evals. A harder/independent data point worth cross-checking against: Aider's April 2024 code-EDITING benchmark separately found this same snapshot scored only 62% (lowest of the GPT-4 family at the time) — not added here since Aider's benchmark isn't the same evaluation as any taxonomy slug (it predates and differs from 'aider-polyglot', which launched later in 2024).
gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09, OpenAI simple-evals (Multilingual Grade School Math).
gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09, OpenAI simple-evals SimpleQA column (short-form factuality / hallucination-rate proxy) — a genuinely hard, non-cherry-picked eval OpenAI's own suite ships this model on.

gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09, Arena-Hard-v0.1 (no style control), win-rate vs. the gpt-4-0314 baseline (fixed at 50.0), GPT-4-Turbo-as-judge pipeline. Official LMArena (formerly LMSYS) archived leaderboard. A style-controlled variant of the same benchmark, added later in 2024, scores this same snapshot at 74.3 in the same archive — noted for completeness but not added as a separate row since it isn't a distinct taxonomy slug.

gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09, Pass@1, official LiveCodeBench leaderboard, current problem time-window 2024-08-01 to 2025-05-01 (454 contamination-controlled problems released after model training cutoffs). A genuinely hard eval: breaks down to Easy 81.0 / Medium 24.8 / Hard 3.1, i.e. the model solves almost none of the Hard-tier problems — included deliberately per the instruction to surface harder evals, not just favorable ones.

gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09, 0-shot CoT, 'assistant' system prompt, OpenAI simple-evals. Distinct from — not a replacement for — the existing 86.5 row already in this file (gpt-4-1106-preview, classic 5-shot, via Telnyx): different snapshot AND different eval protocol, so both are genuine and kept side by side. Headline-score precedence in this repo's derive step still favors the existing 'independent'-sourced row over this 'self-reported' one, so adding it cannot silently inflate the index.

GPT-4-turbo-2024-04-09 snapshot (the same snapshot this repo's gpt-4-turbo.json file already treats as this model's default for other benchmark rows), whole-proof generation with 64 samples, from DeepSeek-Prover (Xin et al., arXiv:2405.14333) Table 1 comparison of state-of-the-art methods on miniF2F. Paper reports 23.0% test / 25.4% valid; this is the test-split figure.

Reported as 'GPT-4-turbo 0409' (matches catalog's default snapshot gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09) in the DeepSeek-Prover paper's Table 1, whole-proof generation on miniF2F-valid (Lean 4). Cross-confirmed at 25.41% by the independent TheoremLlama paper (arXiv:2407.03203).

Reported as gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09 (matches this catalog entry's noted 'current default snapshot'), scored via Shaberi eval framework's Rakuda test (GPT-4-judged, 0-10 absolute scale, 40 Japan-topic questions).

LogicKor official leaderboard, model_id openai/gpt-4-1106-preview (matches catalog's 2023-11-06 GPT-4 Turbo release), default prompt strategy [score corrected during adversarial verify: proposed 9.18 -> 9.1, per verifier: Fetched lk.instruct.kr directly and parsed the embedded logickor_v2_scores dataset (245 records). Exactly two records exist for model_id "openai/gpt-4-1106-preview": (1) prompt_strategy "1-shot", avg=9.095238095238095 (≈9.10); (2) prompt_strategy "cot-1-shot", avg=8.976190476190476 (≈8.98). There is NO "default" prompt-strategy record for this model_id at all — that part of the claimed provenance is fabricated. The claimed score 9.18 does not match either real record. Best real match is the 1-shot avg, ≈9.10 (or 9.09 if truncated rather than rounded to 2dp). A separate, different model_id ("openai/gpt-4-0125-preview", default strategy, avg=9.13) exists in the dataset and could be a source of confusion but is a different snapshot, not GPT-4 Turbo (1106) per the catalog's release date. Recommend updating the result to score=9.10, source note "1-shot prompt strategy" (or 8.98 for cot-1-shot if that variant is preferred), and removing the incorrect "default prompt strategy" characterization.]

verificationNotes: DevDay announcement Nov 6, 2023 confirmed (OpenAI/CNBC); 128K context and $10/$30 per-million pricing confirmed via OpenAI's own DevDay post and current docs. Both result rows (MMLU 86.5 5-shot; Arena Elo 1251) verified literally on the cited Telnyx page (fetched directly, confirmed exact text). No changes made.
